zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Wrey於1445年出生在英國戴文時，她的父親John Waye Robert Wrey34歲，母親Jane Camell29歲。她於1460年與Nicholas Kirkham Sr.在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1480年之後在英國越可爾多卡斯特，埋葬在英國英格蘭米德萊可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Wrey于1445年出生在英国戴文时，她的父亲John Waye Robert Wrey34岁，母亲Jane Camell29岁。她于1460年与Nicholas Kirkham Sr.在英国戴文结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1480年之后在英国越可爾多卡斯特，埋葬在英国英格兰米德萊可。

nameMeanings.name: Wrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Wray Barton (Devon).

2 English: nickname, perhaps ‘the twisted, crooked one’, from Middle English wry(e) ‘awry’, a derivative of Old English wrīgian ‘to turn’. It is possible, however, that le Wrey (1313) is a scribal error for de Wrey, and that the following early bearers belong under (1).

variants.name: Wray
meanings: from Middle English wra (Old Norse vrá) ‘nook, corner of land’, a topographic name for someone who lived at or by a nook, corner of land, or isolated place, or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Wray in Tunstall (Lancashire), Wrea in Kirkham (Lancashire), and High and Low Wreah in Hensingham (Cumberland). The name was taken to Ulster by a Yorkshire family in the reign of Elizabeth I, and was used interchangeably in the counties of Derry and Donegal with Raw, Ray, Rea, and occasionally McRae .
common post-medieval spelling of   Ray  ,   Rae  , or   Ree  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Wrey was born in 1445, in Devon, England, her father, John Waye Robert Wrey, was 34 and her mother, Jane Camell, was 29. She married Nicholas Kirkham Sr. in 1460, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died after 1480, in Doncaster, Yorkshire, England, and was buried in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Wrey est née en 1445, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, John Waye Robert Wrey, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Jane Camell, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas Kirkham Sr. en 1460, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée après 1480, à Doncaster, Everwyck, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Wrey 1445 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Waye Robert Wrey 34 und ihre Mutter Jane Camell 29. Sie hat 1460 in Devon, England Nicholas Kirkham Sr. geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1480 in Doncaster, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Wrey è nata il nel 1445 nel Devon, England, sua padre, John Waye Robert Wrey, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Jane Camell, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Nicholas Kirkham Sr. nel 1460, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 1480, nel Doncaster, Yorkshire, England, e fu sepolta a Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Wreyが1445年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父John Waye Robert Wreyは34歳，母Jane Camellは29歳であった。1460年にイギリスデボンでNicholas Kirkham Sr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1480年以後にイギリスヨークシュアイアドンカスターで死去し，イギリスイギリスミッドレセックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Wrey(이)가 1445년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Waye Robert Wrey(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Camell(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1460년 영국 데폰에서 Nicholas Kirkham Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1480년 이후 영국 요르크실르 돈카스터에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Wrey nasceu em 1445, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Waye Robert Wrey, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Jane Camell, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Nicholas Kirkham Sr. em 1460, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1480, em Doncaster, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Wrey родилась в 1445 г. в местности девон, Англия, её отцу по имени John Waye Robert Wrey было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Jane Camell, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas Kirkham Sr. в 1460 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 1480 г. в местности донкастир, еркшир, Англия и была похоронена в местности миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Wrey nació en 1445, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, John Waye Robert Wrey, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Jane Camell, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Nicholas Kirkham Sr. en 1460, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió después de 1480, en Doncaster, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, y fue sepultada en Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
